title: Add Invoice — request form with saved contacts
what: The VCC's Add Deposit button is now Add Invoice: a full request form (deposit #, amount, send-to, CC, 35-character description) that emails the details to billing@unbridledmedia.com and records the request. Send To autofills from every client contact the platform has ever seen, and new name/email pairs are saved automatically for future invoices. Who to CC is a contact search with an + Add New option — each address becomes a removable pill. The billing email is a configurable automation (Invoice Request to Billing) in the admin Automations dashboard.
where: ProFi → open a project → VCC → Client Deposits → + Add Invoice
how: Open the project's VCC tab and click + Add Invoice in the Client Deposits tile.
Fill Deposit # and Amount.
Start typing in Send To — Name: pick a contact to autofill their email, or type a new name and email (saved for next time).
In Who to CC, search contacts and click to add, or type a new email and hit + Add New — addresses stack as pills with ✕ to remove.
Add the invoice description (35 characters max) and submit — billing@unbridledmedia.com gets the request email and the deposit line appears with the invoice number clickable to review.
Track every request in Reports → Client Invoice Report → Requests view.

title: Invoice Request to Billing — email automation
what: Submitting + Add Invoice in the VCC fires a branded request email to accounting with the deposit #, amount, the invoice's send-to and CC, the 35-character description, and who asked. It's now a managed automation in the admin Automations dashboard: the From and To addresses are editable (default billing@unbridledmedia.com) and the email design can be previewed. There is deliberately no CC field on the automation — the invoice's CC list travels inside the email body for accounting to use on the formal invoice.
where: Hub → Admin → Automations ▸ → Invoice Request to Billing
how: Open the Hub as an admin and click Admin, then Automations ▸.
Find Invoice Request to Billing in the list.
Edit From or To if the request should route somewhere else, and Save.
Click Preview to see exactly what accounting receives.
Every + Add Invoice submission from any project's VCC then emails that address automatically (once SMTP is connected).

title: Client Outstanding Invoice Report
what: One report tracking client invoice requests. The default Unsent view lists every invoice that has been requested from a project's VCC but not yet sent — status pill, project, budget owner, deposit #, description, send-to and CC, amount, and who requested it when — with a running unsent total in the header. The All Requests view adds the full history with ✓ Sent / Unsent status on each.
how: Open Reports from the Hub and click Client Outstanding Invoice Report.
The Unsent view loads first: everything requested but not yet sent, newest first, with the unsent count and dollar total up top.
Switch to All Requests for the full history including sent invoices and their sent dates.
Click any row to jump to that project in ProFi.

title: Project Estimate PDF
what: A client-ready Project Estimate generated straight from the budget on the Unbridled template. It auto-fills the summary block (client, project title, total, today as the sent date, a valid-through date 30 days out, and who prepared it) and rolls every budget line into the template's four investment buckets: scripting/storyboarding plus the coordination fee land in Pre-Production/Creative; shoots, photography, virtual recording, and misc in Production; the post section in Post-Production; and every travel line in Travel. The bottom line always equals the budget's grand total. It opens as a print-ready page with the print dialog popped — save as PDF or print — with no browser headers or footers on the output.
where: ProFi → open a project → Client Budget → Project Estimate PDF
how: Open the project's budget in ProFi and click Client Budget (top right).
Choose Project Estimate PDF in the pop-out.
A print-ready estimate opens in a new tab with the print dialog ready — pick Save as PDF (or a printer).
Check the four bucket subtotals; the total matches the live budget, so update the budget and regenerate if numbers move.

title: Share Budget Options
what: One pop-out for every client-facing view of the budget, opened from the Share Budget Options button on the Finance page. It gathers the Budget Overview, three share views — Itemized with Cost (the full line-by-line budget), Itemized — Subtotals Only (every line item and quantity, with dollars only at section subtotal and total level), and Non-Itemized — Subtotals Only (rolled-up section subtotals, no line detail) — plus the Project Estimate PDF. The last-used share view is remembered, and picking one opens the shareable client page in a new tab.
where: ProFi → open a project's budget → Share Budget Options (top right)
how: Open the project's budget in ProFi and click Share Budget Options.
Pick how the client should see it: Budget Overview, Itemized with Cost, Itemized — Subtotals Only, or Non-Itemized — Subtotals Only.
The client page opens in a new tab — copy its link to send; the choice is saved as the budget's share mode.
Use Project Estimate PDF from the same pop-out for the print-ready estimate document.
